metadata.title: Stablecoin failures, algorithmic unwinds, reserve/collateral shenanigans (USD1, Tether), and market manipulation - the documented record
metadata.frame: Stablecoins are the load-bearing rail of the crypto system and increasingly of the Treasury bid (GENIUS Act). Their failure modes are recurrent and well-documented: (1) ALGORITHMIC stablecoins that hold their peg only by reflexive mint/burn against a volatile sister token - they have failed repeatedly and predictably (Terra/Luna being the largest); (2) RESERVE/COLLATERAL opacity and conflicts in 'collateralized' coins (Tether's reserve history; the USD1/World Liberty Financial conflict); (3) CONTAGION when the bank holding the reserves fails (USDC/SVB). Plus the manipulation layer (wash trading, the Tether-prints-pump-Bitcoin thesis).
metadata.WARNING: Grade discipline. The algorithmic-unwind events are HARD FACTS (on-chain, dated, billions wiped). Tether's reserve misrepresentation is ADJUDICATED (NYAG 2021 settlement, CFTC fine) for the PAST; current full-backing is asserted by attestation, not full audit - graded accordingly. The USD1/World Liberty Financial CONFLICT is documented (Trump-family financial interest + the MGX-Binance settlement); characterizing it as corrupt is interpretation - the financial-interest and the transaction are facts, intent is labeled. The Tether-pumps-Bitcoin thesis (Griffin-Shams) is an ACADEMIC FINDING that is contested, not proven causation. Overlay edges excluded from the proofs.
metadata.method: On-chain records + dated post-mortems; SEC/CFTC/DOJ litigation releases (Terraform/Do Kwon, Tether/Bitfinex, iFinex); NYAG iFinex settlement (Feb 2021); the Griffin & Shams 'Is Bitcoin Really Untethered?' study (J. Finance 2020); World Liberty Financial disclosures + MGX/Binance announcements; Circle 8-K/attestations. Each cell graded.

algorithmic_unwinds.name: TerraUSD (UST) / Luna
algorithmic_unwinds.mechanism: UST held its $1 peg only by being mint/burn-arbitraged against LUNA; the Anchor Protocol paid an unsustainable ~20% yield to park UST, concentrating reflexive demand. A large UST outflow broke the peg; the arbitrage minted hyperinflationary LUNA, and ~$40-60B of value evaporated in days (the 'death spiral').
algorithmic_unwinds.fallout: Triggered the 2022 crypto contagion (Three Arrows Capital, Celsius, Voyager insolvencies). Do Kwon (Terraform Labs) charged by the SEC and DOJ with fraud; arrested in Montenegro (2023), extradition fought; Terraform settled with the SEC for ~$4.5B (2024).

algorithmic_unwinds.name: Iron Finance (IRON / TITAN)
algorithmic_unwinds.mechanism: Partially-algorithmic stablecoin; a bank-run dynamic collapsed TITAN from ~$60 to ~$0 in a day - the 'first large-scale crypto bank run.' Mark Cuban was a public holder who got burned and then called for stablecoin regulation.

algorithmic_unwinds.name: Basis Cash / Empty Set Dollar / others
algorithmic_unwinds.mechanism: A wave of 'seigniorage-style' algorithmic stablecoins (Basis Cash co-founded pseudonymously by Do Kwon) that all de-pegged and failed to recover - a repeated, predictable pattern BEFORE Terra, which makes Terra's later collapse less a surprise than a scaling of a known failure mode.

algorithmic_unwinds.name: USDD (Tron)
algorithmic_unwinds.mechanism: Justin Sun's over-collateralized/algorithmic hybrid on Tron; lost its peg during the 2022 stress and has traded below $1 for extended periods, propped by TRON DAO reserves. A live example of a persistently under-pegged 'stablecoin.'

algorithmic_unwinds.name: DEI (Deus Finance), Neutrino USD (USDN, Waves), Fei, Beanstalk
algorithmic_unwinds.mechanism: Further algorithmic/partially-collateralized failures and de-pegs in 2022 (USDN/Waves de-peg amid manipulation allegations; Beanstalk drained by a flash-loan governance attack). The category's failure rate is the point.

points.detail: Tether/Bitfinex (iFinex) settled with the NY Attorney General for $18.5M over claims that Tether misrepresented that USDT was fully backed and that Bitfinex covered an ~$850M shortfall with Tether reserves. Adjudicated: reserves were NOT always fully cash-backed as represented.
$41M CFTC penalty for untrue/misleading statements that USDT was fully backed; the CFTC found Tether held sufficient fiat reserves only part of the time over 2016-2018.

Tether moved from heavy commercial paper toward US Treasuries; now publishes attestations (not full audits) showing large T-bill holdings - making it a notable marginal Treasury buyer (the GENIUS/stablecoin-Treasury-rail thesis), while full third-party AUDIT remains absent.

reserve_collateral_issues.name: World Liberty Financial / USD1
reserve_collateral_issues.onchain_verified_2026_06: Verified against on-chain data this pass (Arkham Intelligence, surfaced by Forbes/DL News; Etherscan).

points.detail: World Liberty Financial (WLFI) is a DeFi venture in which the Trump family holds a large financial interest; it issues the USD1 stablecoin, launched Mar 2025 on Ethereum + BNB Chain. Ethereum contract 0x8d0d000ee44948fc98c9b98a4fa4921476f08b0d ('World Liberty Financial USD', ~44k holders as of Jun 2026). Custodied by BitGo Trust; backed (per issuer) by cash + short-duration US T-bills via government money-market funds. A sitting president's family profiting from a dollar token while the administration sets stablecoin policy (GENIUS Act) is an unprecedented direct conflict.

Abu Dhabi's MGX made a ~$2B investment into Binance settled in USD1 (announced Token2049, May 1 2025, by WLFI co-founder Zach Witkoff - son of envoy Steve Witkoff) - the largest investment ever paid in stablecoins. The mint and transfer are VERIFIABLE ON-CHAIN; the deal placed a large share of USD1 reserves under Binance's custody and generated reserve interest income for WLFI (the Trump-family beneficiary).

Per Arkham Intelligence (Forbes Feb-2026; DL News), BINANCE CUSTODIES ~87-89% OF ALL USD1 IN CIRCULATION - roughly $4.7B of a ~$5.4B supply. A single exchange holds nearly the entire float of the president's-family stablecoin. This is the 'USD1 collateral issue documented on-chain': USD1 is not a broadly distributed dollar token but an instrument whose reserves/float are concentrated at one counterparty (Binance), into which a $2B Gulf-sovereign flow was routed, with interest accruing to WLFI. The concentration itself is the on-chain fact; its characterization as engineered conflict is labeled interpretation.
USD1 expanded into the Cantor (Lutnick-linked) network and additional venues through 2025-26 - tying the token's distribution to a Commerce-Secretary-linked firm (Lutnick already profiled).

Binance founder Changpeng Zhao received a presidential pardon (2025) while Binance custodied ~87-89% of USD1 and had taken the MGX USD1 settlement - the proximity of a pardon to an entity central to the president's-family stablecoin is a documented sequence; causal corruption is alleged, not adjudicated.
points.grade: fact (the sequence) + interpretation labeled (left contested, not asserted)

points.detail: $3.3B of USDC's reserves were held at Silicon Valley Bank; when SVB failed, USDC fell to ~$0.87 until the FDIC systemic-risk backstop made depositors whole. A 'fully-reserved' stablecoin still broke because the reserve BANK broke - the bank-failure/stablecoin-failure linkage.

manipulation_layer.detail: Griffin & Shams ('Is Bitcoin Really Untethered?', Journal of Finance 2020) found USDT issuance was timed with Bitcoin price support during the 2017 run, consistent with manipulation by a single large actor. Influential but CONTESTED - it shows correlation/timing patterns, not adjudicated manipulation.
manipulation_layer.grade: contested (academic finding, not a legal verdict)

manipulation_layer.detail: Multiple studies and enforcement actions (incl. against unregulated offshore venues) document large fractions of reported crypto volume as wash trades, inflating apparent liquidity - relevant to how stablecoin/altcoin 'depth' is overstated.

Alameda Research acted as a dominant market-maker and (per the SDNY case) used FTX customer funds; the FTX/Alameda nexus is an adjudicated fraud (SBF convicted Nov 2023). The market-structure lesson: a single actor as exchange + market-maker + token issuer is the manipulation-enabling configuration.
The Waves/USDN de-peg (2022) drew allegations of price manipulation around the WAVES token and reserve mechanics - documented allegations, not adjudicated.

custody_concentration_monitor.summary: #65 - a MONITORING FRAMEWORK for stablecoin custody/float concentration as a systemic-risk gauge, generalized from the documented cases. The current readings are fact (on-chain / attestation, Jun-2026); the danger thresholds are a labeled model. The lesson each case teaches: concentration converts an idiosyncratic counterparty failure into a peg break.

metrics.measures: share of a coin's circulating supply held/custodied at one venue
metrics.danger_threshold: >50% at one counterparty = single point of failure; >80% = the token IS that counterparty's instrument
metrics.flags: a venue failure/freeze (or a pardon-for-access dynamic) can break the peg or trap the float

metrics.measures: concentration of the backing assets at one bank/custodian
metrics.current_reading: USDC had $3.3B at SVB (Mar-2023) -> depeg to ~$0.87 when SVB failed; USD1 reserves via BitGo Trust + gov MMFs
metrics.danger_threshold: any single uninsured bank holding a material reserve share
metrics.flags: the SVB lesson - a 'fully-reserved' coin still breaks if the reserve BANK breaks (the stablecoin<->bank-failure linkage)

metrics.metric: ISSUER concentration of the Treasury bid
metrics.measures: how much of the stablecoin-Treasury rail rests on one issuer
metrics.current_reading: Tether >$100B T-bills (~the 18th-largest external US-debt holder); single offshore issuer
metrics.danger_threshold: one issuer > a sovereign-scale share of the marginal T-bill bid
metrics.flags: a single-issuer run is a Treasury-market event (ties macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail + spec-cross-system-contagion B1)

metrics.measures: assurance quality of the reserve claim
metrics.current_reading: Tether: attestations, no full third-party audit; USD1: issuer disclosures + on-chain
metrics.danger_threshold: no full audit on a sovereign-scale issuer
metrics.flags: the NYAG/CFTC precedent - 'fully backed' was adjudicated false for Tether's past; attestation != audit

metrics.metric: REDEMPTION-ACCESS concentration (who can redeem at par)
metrics.measures: breadth of authorized at-par redeemers vs retail
metrics.current_reading: primary redemption typically limited to authorized participants; retail exits via secondary market
metrics.flags: retail can't redeem at par in a run -> the peg is defended by a few, broken by the many

metrics.measures: share of supply on a single blockchain
metrics.current_reading: USD1 launched Ethereum + BNB Chain; most coins skew to 1-2 chains
metrics.flags: a chain halt/exploit freezes redemption for that share

custody_concentration_monitor.synthesis: Across every documented failure the common variable is CONCENTRATION: USDC/SVB (reserve-custodian), Terra (reflexive single-mechanism), USD1 (87-89% float at Binance), Tether (single-issuer Treasury bid + attestation-only). A custody-concentration monitor is therefore the cheapest early-warning gauge: track the six metrics above and the danger is legible BEFORE the peg breaks. USD1 is the live red-flag case - a president's-family token whose float is ~87-89% at one pardoned-founder exchange, into which a $2B Gulf-sovereign flow was routed, with reserve interest accruing to the issuer. The concentration is the on-chain fact; the engineered-conflict reading is labeled.
custody_concentration_monitor.grade: current readings FACT (on-chain/attestation Jun-2026); thresholds are a labeled monitoring model

edges.note: OVERLAY: president's-family stablecoin amid stablecoin policy-setting
OVERLAY: documented sequence; corruption alleged not adjudicated

edges.raw_instrument: Binance custodies ~87-89% of all USD1 supply (~$4.7B/$5.4B) - Arkham on-chain

limits: Algorithmic unwinds and adjudicated cases (Terra/Do Kwon, Tether NYAG/CFTC, FTX/SBF) are HARD facts. The USD1/WLFI conflict is documented as a financial-interest + transaction; the corruption characterization (incl. the CZ-pardon proximity) is LABELED interpretation, deliberately left contested rather than asserted as verdict. The Tether-pumps-Bitcoin thesis is a contested academic finding, not proof. Current Tether full-backing rests on attestation, not audit - stated as such. Persons Do Kwon and Justin Sun are added to the Persons tab; SBF and the Gulf/Trump nodes already exist.
